text: First Starship V3 launch later this week! https://t.co/JFX4CrSfnY
summary: First Starship V3 launch later this week!
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces an upcoming Starship V3 launch scheduled for later in the week, signaling progress on SpaceX's next-generation fully reusable super heavy-lift launch vehicle. This represents a key engineering milestone in SpaceX's iterative development approach, demonstrating the company's rapid advancement toward operational capability for deep space missions, lunar transport, and Mars colonization objectives.
embed_html:
First Starship V3 launch later this week! pic.twitter.com/JFX4CrSfnY

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 19, 2026

text: Join tunneling – the underground movement https://t.co/SGNfIyHEE9
ai_summary: Elon Musk promotes The Boring Company's tunneling technology and services through a recruitment/engagement call-to-action. The post highlights the company's core business of underground infrastructure development, positioning tunneling as both a literal transportation solution and metaphorical 'underground movement,' demonstrating how Musk leverages social media to drive awareness and recruitment for his ventures beyond Tesla and SpaceX.
embed_html:
Join tunneling – the underground movement https://t.co/SGNfIyHEE9

text: Release notes for Grok Build will be published daily https://t.co/7EIwS3tgTB
summary: Release notes for Grok Build will be published daily
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that xAI will publish daily release notes for Grok Build, indicating an accelerated development and transparency cadence for the AI assistant product. This signals xAI's commitment to rapid iteration and public accountability in AI product development, reflecting a startup-style deployment velocity approach to competing in the generative AI market.
embed_html:
Release notes for Grok Build will be published daily https://t.co/7EIwS3tgTB

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 20, 2026

text:
@karankendre Anthropic will not be destroyed. Their AI+harness goes far beyond coding and Opus 4.7 is still better than Composer 2.5, albeit a lot more expensive. 

Cursor is however an important piece of the puzzle to make Grok much better.
summary: @karankendre Anthropic will not be destroyed. Their AI+harness goes far beyond coding and Opus 4.7 is still better than Composer 2.5, albeit a lot more expensive. Cursor is however an important piece of the puzzle to make Grok much better.

parent.text: Cursor did a collab with SpaceX to destroy Anthropic. Composer 2.5 has benchmark scores close to Opus 4.7, and it costs 10x less
parent.name: Karan

ai_summary: A developer discusses Cursor's AI coding assistant (Composer 2.5) achieving near-parity performance with Anthropic's Opus 4.7 at 1/10th the cost, prompting speculation about competitive threats. Elon responds with a competitive analysis clarifying that while Cursor is valuable for improving Grok's capabilities, Anthropic's broader AI+harness approach and superior Opus model remain defensible despite cost disadvantages—illustrating how specialized tools can coexist with general-purpose AI platforms in an evolving market.
embed_html:
Anthropic will not be destroyed. Their AI+harness goes far beyond coding and Opus 4.7 is still better than Composer 2.5, albeit a lot more expensive.

Cursor is however an important piece of the puzzle to make Grok much better.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 19, 2026
